articles.content:
Royalty Pharma (RPRX) director gets equity grant in lieu of cash

Rhea-AI Filing Summary

Bassler Bonnie L reported acquisition or exercise transactions in this Form 4 filing.

Royalty Pharma plc director Bonnie L. Bassler received an award of 689 Class A Ordinary Shares, valued at $54.3998 per share. The shares were issued under the company’s 2020 Independent Director Equity Incentive Plan in lieu of a quarterly cash retainer of $37,500.

After this equity award, Bassler directly holds 79,181 Class A Ordinary Shares. This transaction reflects compensation paid in stock rather than cash, and does not represent an open-market purchase or sale.

articles.title: Shreedhar Spinners IPO listing: Shares hit upper price band after a positive debut; details here
articles.summary: Shares of Shreedhar Spinners made a positive debut and extended gains to hit their upper price band on the NSE SME on Wednesday, 1 July. Shreedhar Spinners IPO opened for public subscription on Tuesday, 23 June, and concluded on Thursday, 25 June.
articles.content:
Shreedhar Spinners IPO listing: Shares hit upper price band after a positive debut; details here

Shares of Shreedhar Spinners made a positive debut and extended gains to hit their upper price band on the NSE SME on Wednesday, 1 July. Shreedhar Spinners IPO opened for public subscription on Tuesday, 23 June, and concluded on Thursday, 25 June.

Shreedhar Spinners IPO listing: Shares of Shreedhar Spinners made a positive debut and extended gains to hit their upper price band on the NSE SME on Wednesday, 1 July. Shreedhar Spinners' share price was listed at ₹54.40, up 2.6% from the issue price of ₹53 and rose 7.74% from the IPO price to hit its upper circuit of ₹57.10.

The SME stock's listing was slightly better than expected, as the last grey market premium (GMP) indicated the stock could see a flat listing.

Shreedhar Spinners IPO details

Shreedhar Spinners IPO opened for public subscription on Tuesday, 23 June, and concluded on Thursday, 25 June, with an overall subscription of nearly 7 times.

The IPO was an entirely fresh issue of 57,88,000 shares, priced at ₹51 to ₹53 per share, and aimed to raise about ₹30.68 crore, which the company intends to use for meeting working capital requirements, the purchase of machinery in the existing manufacturing facility at Amravati, Maharashtra, and for general corporate purposes.

Shreedhar Spinners is engaged in the manufacturing of compact spun cotton yarn of various counts at its manufacturing facility in Amravati, Maharashtra.

The company operates exclusively in the business-to-business (B2B) segment, supplying its products to textile manufacturers, yarn exporters, traders and fabric processors.

The company's revenue from operations for FY24, FY25, and FY26 stood at ₹126.14 crore, ₹134.27 crore, and ₹146.37 crore, respectively. Profit for FY24 was ₹3.35 crore, which rose to ₹3.42 crore in FY25, and to ₹6.17 crore in FY26.

Disclaimer: This story is for educational purposes only and does not constitute investment advice. The views and recommendations expressed are those of individual analysts or broking firms, not Mint. We advise investors to consult with certified experts before making any investment decisions, as market conditions can change rapidly and circumstances may vary.

articles.title: Super Group (NYSE: SGHC) CTO settles RSUs and sells shares for taxes
articles.summary: Super Group's CTO, Ben David Alon, settled 38,915 restricted stock units (RSUs) into common stock on June 30, 2026, from a grant of 116,747 RSUs on June 12, 2026. Simultaneously, he sold 7,801 shares of common stock at $13.74 per share to cover tax withholding obligations. Aft...
articles.content:
Super Group (NYSE: SGHC) CTO settles RSUs and sells shares for taxes

Rhea-AI Filing Summary

Super Group (SGHC) Ltd’s Chief Technology Officer, Ben David Alon, reported routine equity compensation activity. On June 12, 2026, he was granted 116,747 restricted stock units (RSUs). Of these, 38,915 RSUs were settled into common stock on June 30, 2026.

On the same date, he sold 7,801 shares of common stock at $13.74 per share solely to cover tax withholding obligations from the RSU vesting. After these transactions, he held 31,114 shares of common stock directly and 77,832 RSUs scheduled to vest in equal installments on March 31, 2027 and March 31, 2028.

The remaining RSUs will vest in two equal installments on March 31, 2027, and March 31, 2028. Upon vesting, the RSUs will be settled on a one-for-one basis in shares of the Issuer's common stock or the cash value thereof, at the election of the Issuer Mr. Ben-David sold 7,801 shares of the Issuer's common stock upon the partial vesting of RSUs granted to him on June 30, 2026, solely to satisfy tax withholding obligations incurred upon vesting.

articles.title: Papa John’s (Nasdaq: PZZA) appoints Chris Collins interim CFO
articles.summary: Papa John's International, Inc. announced that Chris Collins has been appointed interim Chief Financial Officer, effective June 30, 2026. This follows the resignation of Ravi Thanawala, who is leaving to become CFO at another public company. Collins, previously Senior Vice Pre...
articles.content:
Papa John’s (Nasdaq: PZZA) appoints Chris Collins interim CFO

Rhea-AI Filing Summary

Papa John’s International, Inc. announced a planned Chief Financial Officer transition. Ravi Thanawala will resign as CFO and President, North America effective June 30, 2026, to become CFO at another public company, and will remain in an advisory role through July 31, 2026. The company states his resignation is not due to any disagreement regarding operations, policies or practices and that he will not receive severance.

The board appointed Chris Collins, Senior Vice President of Corporate Finance and Principal Accounting Officer, as interim CFO effective June 30, 2026. For the period he serves in this role, his annual base salary increases to $415,000 and his target annual bonus opportunity rises to 75% of base salary. Papa John’s has begun a search for a permanent CFO and reassigned North America operations responsibilities to Marc Richard. The company also plans to release second quarter 2026 results before market open on August 6, 2026, followed by an earnings conference call at 8:00 a.m. ET.

Positive

  • None.

Negative

  • None.

Insights

Planned CFO change with experienced internal interim, limited immediate disruption signal.

Papa John’s is managing an executive transition as Ravi Thanawala departs as CFO and President, North America to take another public-company CFO role. The company explicitly notes no disagreements and no severance, suggesting an orderly, non-adversarial exit.

They have elevated seasoned insider Chris Collins, already Principal Accounting Officer and former interim CFO, to serve again as interim CFO with adjusted pay of $415,000 base salary and a 75% bonus target. His long tenure in finance roles may support continuity while the board conducts its permanent CFO search.

The release also flags upcoming Q2 2026 results on August 6, 2026, where Collins will appear on the earnings call. How he communicates strategy and financial priorities on that call will provide additional insight into leadership style and alignment with the company’s stated transformation and efficiency objectives.

articles.title: Papa John's Stock Slips On CFO Surprise Exit
articles.summary: Papa John's International (PZZA) stock saw a 5.11% decline in after-hours trading following the immediate resignation of CFO Ravi Thanawala. While Thanawala will remain in an advisory role until July 31, 2026, the absence of a permanent successor creates uncertainty for invest...
articles.content:
• PZZA stock is down. See the chart and price action here.

The search for a replacement is underway and the company noted that Thanawala will remain in an advisory role through July 31, 2026. This transition period provides some reassurance, as it allows for knowledge transfer and operational stability.

However, the absence of a permanent successor adds pressure, and investors often prefer immediate clarity on leadership succession.

Market reaction will likely hinge on how quickly Papa John’s fills the role and the credentials of the incoming executive. Until then, short-term volatility in the stock may persist as investors reassess risk tied to leadership change.

PZZA Stock Price Activity: Papa John’s shares were down 5.11% at $34.89 during after-hours trading on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Over the past month, Papa John’s has gained about 1.7% versus a 1.1% decline in the S&P 500 and is down roughly 5% year-to-date compared to the index’s 8.9% gain.

articles.title: Al Harper takes interim CEO role at Rocky Mountain Chocolate (Nasdaq: RMCF)
articles.summary: Rocky Mountain Chocolate Factory, Inc. (Nasdaq: RMCF) has appointed former Board member Al Harper as Interim Chief Executive Officer and Principal Executive Officer for a 180-day term, effective immediately. The company expects no changes to its strategic direction or day-to-d...
articles.content:
Al Harper takes interim CEO role at Rocky Mountain Chocolate (Nasdaq: RMCF)

Rhea-AI Filing Summary

Rocky Mountain Chocolate Factory, Inc. appointed former Board member Al (Allen C.) Harper as Interim Chief Executive Officer and Principal Executive Officer, effective immediately, for a 180-day term. The Board stated it does not anticipate changes to the company’s strategic direction or day-to-day operations as a result of this leadership transition.

The company highlighted Mr. Harper’s prior service on its Board, his experience with franchised businesses and consumer brands, and his longstanding connection to the Durango community. Rocky Mountain Chocolate Factory and its franchisees and licensees operate over 250 branded stores across the United States, with several international locations.

Positive

  • None.

Negative

  • None.

Insights

RMCF installs an experienced former director as interim CEO for 180 days while affirming its current strategy.

Rocky Mountain Chocolate Factory appointed former Board member Al Harper as Interim Chief Executive Officer and Principal Executive Officer for a 180-day term, effective immediately. The company emphasizes his experience in franchising, consumer brands and public-company leadership.

Management and the Board state they do not expect changes to strategic priorities or day-to-day operations, signaling a continuity approach during this transition. The interim structure suggests a defined window for leadership evaluation or a potential longer-term decision, though no further details are provided in this disclosure.

articles.title: Rush Enterprises, Inc. Announces Dual Listing on Nasdaq Texas Exchange
articles.summary: Rush Enterprises, Inc. announced a dual listing of its common stock on the new Nasdaq Texas exchange, while maintaining its primary listing on the Nasdaq Global Select Market. This move, which will not impact investors' ability to trade the stock on the Nasdaq Global Select Ma...
articles.content:
NEW BRAUNFELS, Texas, June 30, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Rush Enterprises, Inc. (NASDAQ: RUSHA & RUSHB), which operates the largest network of commercial vehicle dealerships in North America, today announced the dual listing of its common stock on the Nasdaq Texas, LLC ("Nasdaq Texas”) exchange. The Company will maintain its primary listing on the Nasdaq Global Select Market, and the dual listing will not affect investors’ ability to buy or sell the Company’s common stock on the Nasdaq Global Select Market. Nasdaq Texas is a newly established dual listing exchange created to broaden capital access for companies with strong ties to Texas. The Company’s common stock will continue to trade under the ticker symbols "RUSHA” and "RUSHB,” and the dual listing will not involve the issuance of new shares or change the Company’s governance structure or reporting obligations. The Company expects the listing and trading of its common stock on Nasdaq Texas to commence on July 1, 2026.

"While Rush Enterprises’ mission is to deliver best-in-class commercial vehicle solutions that enable our customers to keep the economy up and running across all of North America, our dealership roots are in Texas and our headquarters has always been here,” said W.M. "Rusty” Rush, Chairman, Chief Executive Officer and President of Rush Enterprises, Inc. "We have 55 locations in Texas, employ approximately 2400 individuals in Texas and serve countless customers across the Lone Star State. We have grown along with our home state and are proud to be a part of the opportunity and growth that Nasdaq Texas represents,” added Rush.

About Rush Enterprises, Inc.

Rush Enterprises, Inc. is the premier solutions provider to the commercial vehicle industry. The Company owns and operates Rush Truck Centers, the largest network of commercial vehicle dealerships in North America, with more than 160 locations in 24 states and Ontario, Canada. These vehicle centers, strategically located in high traffic areas on or near major highways throughout the United States and Ontario, Canada, represent truck and bus manufacturers, including Peterbilt, International, Hino, Isuzu, Ford, IC Bus and Blue Bird. They offer an integrated approach to meeting customer needs - from sales of new and used vehicles to aftermarket parts, service and body shop operations plus financing, insurance, and leasing and rental solutions. Rush Enterprises' operations also provide CNG fuel systems (through its investment in Cummins Clean Fuel Technologies, Inc.), telematics products and other vehicle technologies, as well as vehicle modification and up-fitting, chrome accessories and tires. For more information, please visit us at www.rushtruckcenters.com and www.rushenterprises.com, on X @rushtruckcenter, Facebook.com/rushtruckcenters and www.linkedin.com/company/rushenterprises-inc..

Certain statements contained in this release, including those concerning current and projected market conditions, sales forecasts, market share forecast and anticipated demand for the Company’s services, are "forward-looking” statements (as such term is defined in the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995). Such forward-looking statements only speak as of the date of this release and the Company assumes no obligation to update the information included in this release. Because such statements include risks and uncertainties, actual results may differ materially from those expressed or implied by such forward-looking statements. Important factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed or implied by such forward-looking statements are disclosed in our annual report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025.

Contact:

Rush Enterprises, Inc., New Braunfels

Steven L. Keller, 830-302-5226

articles.title: Rain Enhancement Technologies Signs $3.5 Million Sales Agreement With Needham & Company, LLC – 8-K SEC Filing June 2026 – Minichart
articles.summary: Rain Enhancement Technologies Holdco, Inc. has announced a sales agreement with Needham & Company, LLC, potentially allowing the company to sell up to $3.5 million in Class A common stock. This move, detailed in an 8-K SEC filing on June 30, 2026, could impact its capital stru...
articles.content:
Rain Enhancement Technologies Holdco, Inc. Announces Sales Agreement with Needham & Company, LLC

Key Points for Investors

  • Date of Announcement: June 30, 2026
  • Form: Current Report on Form 8-K filed with the SEC
  • Material Event: Entry into a Sales Agreement
  • Sales Agent: Needham & Company, LLC
  • Security: Class A common stock, \$0.0001 par value per share
  • Aggregate Offering Price: Up to \$3,513,524 of Class A common stock
  • Trading Symbols: RAIN (Class A common stock), RAINW (Redeemable warrants)
  • Exchange: Nasdaq Stock Market LLC
  • Emerging Growth Company: Yes

Detailed Overview of the Sales Agreement

Rain Enhancement Technologies Holdco, Inc. (“Company”) has entered into a Sales Agreement with Needham & Company, LLC (“Sales Agent”).
Under this arrangement, the Company may, at its discretion, offer and sell shares of its Class A common stock via the Sales Agent, with total proceeds not to exceed \$3,513,524.

The issuance and sale of these shares will be conducted through the Company’s registration statement on Form S-3, which was filed with the SEC on June 30, 2026. This includes a base prospectus and a prospectus supplement specific to the sale. The Sales Agreement is attached as Exhibit 1.1 to the current report and is incorporated by reference.

The representations, warranties, and covenants within the Sales Agreement are made solely for the benefit of the parties involved and may be subject to negotiated limitations. Investors are advised to review the Sales Agreement in conjunction with the Company’s periodic SEC filings.

Potential Impact on Shareholders

  • Price-Sensitive Information:
    The announcement of a potential offering of up to \$3.5 million in Class A common stock may be material for shareholders, as it could lead to dilution of existing shares depending on the number of shares issued. The sale of shares at market prices, especially if made in large volumes, may also impact the trading price of Rain Enhancement Technologies’ stock.
  • Forward-Looking Statements:
    The Company cautions that there is no guarantee it will sell any shares under the Sales Agreement, or that sales will occur at anticipated prices. Effectiveness of the Registration Statement, use of proceeds, and other actions are subject to risks and uncertainties, including market conditions and regulatory approvals.
  • Emerging Growth Status:
    The Company is classified as an “emerging growth company” as defined by Rule 405 of the Securities Act of 1933, which may allow it to utilize certain reduced disclosure requirements and extended transition periods for new accounting standards, although it has not elected to use the extended transition period for complying with new standards.
  • Securities Registered:
    • Class A common stock, par value \$0.0001 per share (Trading Symbol: RAIN)
    • Redeemable warrants, each exercisable for one share of Class A common stock at \$11.50 per share (Trading Symbol: RAINW)

    Both securities are listed on the Nasdaq Stock Market LLC.

Additional Information and Risks

The Company notes that this Form 8-K does not constitute an offer to sell or a solicitation of an offer to buy shares of common stock discussed herein in any state or jurisdiction where such an offer would be unlawful.

Risks highlighted by the Company include:

  • Potential adverse effects on business from disclosures in this Current Report
  • Volatility of the Company’s stock price following the announcement
  • Possibility of new legal proceedings
  • Risks discussed in the Company’s Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2025, and other SEC filings

The Company disclaims any obligation to update forward-looking statements contained in this report except as required by law.

Conclusion

The entry into a Sales Agreement with Needham & Company, LLC to potentially raise up to \$3.5 million through the sale of common stock is a significant event for Rain Enhancement Technologies Holdco, Inc. shareholders. This move could impact both the capital structure and the trading price of the Company’s securities. Investors should closely monitor subsequent filings, prospectus supplements, and sales activity for further developments.

Disclaimer: The information provided above is a summary of Rain Enhancement Technologies Holdco, Inc.’s Form 8-K filing dated June 30, 2026. This article is intended for informational purposes only and does not constitute investment advice or an offer to buy or sell securities. Investors should refer to official SEC filings and consult with their financial advisors before making investment decisions. The Company undertakes no obligation to update any forward-looking statements contained herein except as required by law.
